questions.questionText: Out of the following alkenes, the one which will produce tertiary butyl alcohol on acid catalysed hydration is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: The substituted alkene on acidic hydrolysis produces a tertiary alcohol according to Markownikoff's addition where the negative part of the addendum goes to the carbon atom having least number of hydrogen atoms.

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): p-methoxyphenol is a stronger acid than $p$-nitrophenol.

Reason (R): Methoxy group shows + I effect whereas nitro group shows -I effect.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
 Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
 Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
 Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: $P$-nitrophenol is a more acidic than $p$-methoxyphenol. The nitro group present in para position exerts -R effect and increases acidic character. Methoxy group in para position exerts $+R$ effect and decreases acidic character.

questions.questionText: Which of the following species can act as the strongest base?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: The stronger base is a conjugate form of its weak acid. ROH is the weakest acid. Also, the positive Inductive effect of the alkyl group in $\mathrm{RO}^{-}$ increases the negative charge and makes it a very strong base.

questions.questionText: The specific sequence in which amino acids are arranged in a protein is called its
options:
Primary structure
 Secondary structure
 Tertiary structure
 Quaternary structure
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The specific sequence in which amino acids are arranged in a protein is called its primary structure.

The primary structure of a protein refers to the linear sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain. It is determined by the genetic information encoded in DNA, and it ultimately dictates the protein's higher level structures and function. This sequence is held together by peptide bonds, which link the carboxyl group of one amino acid to the amino group of the next.

Here's an illustration of a simple linear sequence of amino acids, which represents a protein's primary structure:

$$ \text{Amino acid}_1 - \text{Amino acid}_2 - \text{Amino acid}_3 - \cdots - \text{Amino acid}_n $$

Changes or mutations in the primary structure can significantly impact the folding and function of the protein, potentially leading to various functional anomalies or diseases.

questions.questionText: The rate of a reaction increases sixteen times when the concentration of the reactant increases four times. The order of the reaction is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The rate of a reaction and its relation to the concentration of reactants can be described by the rate law:

$$ \text{Rate} = k [\text{A}]^n $$

where:

$\text{Rate}$ is the rate of the reaction,

$k$ is the rate constant,

$[\text{A}]$ is the concentration of the reactant, and

$n$ is the order of the reaction with respect to reactant A.

From the problem statement, the rate increases sixteen times when the concentration increases four times. This relationship can be expressed as:

$$ 16 \times \text{Rate}_0 = k (4[\text{A}_0])^n $$

where $\text{Rate}_0$ is the initial rate and $[\text{A}_0]$ is the initial concentration.

Since the initial rate is $\text{Rate}_0 = k [\text{A}_0]^n$, substituting and simplifying gives:

$$ 16 \times [\text{A}_0]^n = (4[\text{A}_0])^n $$

Dividing each side by $([\text{A}_0]^n)$:

$$ 16 = 4^n $$

Since $4^2 = 16$, it follows that $n = 2$.

Therefore, the order of the reaction is 2.0.

Option B is correct.

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): The units of rate constant of a zero order reaction and rate of reaction are the same.

Reason (R): In zero order reaction, the rate of reaction is independent of the concentration of reactants.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
 Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
 Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
 Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: The rate of a zero-order reaction does not depend on the concentration of the reactants. Therefore, the rate of a zero-order reaction is always equal to the rate constant of that specific reaction.

questions.questionText: Transition metals are known to make interstitial compounds. Formation of interstitial compounds makes the transition metal
options:
more hard
more soft
more ductile
more metallic
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Though the chemical properties do not change, when Transition metals form interstitial compounds they become more hard.

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): Zr and Hf are of almost similar atomic radii.

Reason (R): This is due to lanthanoid contraction.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
 Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
 Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
 Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Both Zr and Hf have same atomic radii and this is due to the lanthanoid contraction.

(c) Reaction of phenol with bromine water.

(b) According to Markovnikov's Rule -

questions.questionText:
(a) An organic compound (A) with the molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_9 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}$ forms 2, 4-DNP derivative, reduces Fehling solution and undergoes Cannizzaro reaction. On vigorous oxidation, it gives 1, 2-benzene dicarboxylic acid.

(i) Identify the compound (A) and write its IUPAC name.

(ii) Write the reaction of compound (A) with

(1) 2,4-Dinitrophenyl hydrazine and

(2) Fehling solution

(iii) Write the equation of compound (A) when it undergoes Cannizzaro reaction.

OR

(b) (i) Account for the following:

(1) The alpha ( $\alpha$ )-hydrogens of aldehydes and ketones are acidic in nature.

(2) Oxidation of aldehydes is easier than ketones.

(ii) Arrange the following in :

(1) Decreasing reactivity towards nucleophilic addition reaction propanal, acetone, benzaldehyde.

(2) Increasing order of boiling point: Propane, Ethanol, Dimethylether, Propanal

(iii) Give simple chemical test to distinguish between Benzoic acid and Benzaldehyde.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(a) (i) 2-Ethyl benzaldehyde

(ii) (1)

(2) Fehling's reaction does not happen with benzaldehyde.

(iii)

Cannizzaro reaction does not affect the alkyl substituent. Same products are formed.

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{CHO}\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right)+\mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right) \\ & \mathrm{COONa}+{ }^{+}+\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_4\left(\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3\right) \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH} \end{aligned}$$

OR

(b) (i) (1) The hydrogen at alpha position of carbonyl group is acidic as it experiences -I effect of the carbonyl group. The proton can be abstracted by a base and the resulting anion is stabilised by ketoenol tautomerism

(2) Aldehydes are more reactive than ketones and can be easily oxidized. Aldehydes $(-\mathrm{CHO})$ have a hydrogen atom attached to carbonyl carbon which can easily be abstracted. Ketones lack this proton, hence they oxidise only under extreme conditions where $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{C}$ bonds are broken.

(ii) (1) propanal $>$ acetone $>$ Benzaldehyde

(2) Propane < dimethyl ether < propanal < ethanol

(iii) Benzoic acid reacts with ethanol in the presence of dilute sulphuric acid to form sweet smelling ester Ethyl benzoate. This reaction is not given by benzaldehyde.

questions.questionText: A compound ' X ' with molecular formula $\mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{H}_9 \mathrm{~N}$ reacts with $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$ to give a solid, insoluble in alkali. Identify ' X ' and give the IUPAC name of the product. Write the reaction involved.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Since the compound formed on reaction with $\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{SO}_2 \mathrm{Cl}$ is insoluble in alkali, the amine is a secondary amine. The structure of the compound $X$ is $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{NHCH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3$. IUPAC is N - Methyl ethanamine.

questions.questionText: Which of the following cell was used in Apollo space programme?
options:
Mercury cell
 $\mathrm{H}_2-\mathrm{O}_2$ fuel cell
 Dry cell
 Ni-Cd cell
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: It was chosen for its ability to generate electricity by combining hydrogen and oxygen from hydrogen peroxide, producing water as a byproduct. This type of fuel cell provided a reliable and efficient source of power for the spacecraft during the Apollo missions.

questions.questionText: Dilution affects both conductivity as well as molar conductivity. Effect of dilution on both is as follows:
options:
both increase with dilution.
 both decrease with dilution.
 conductivity increases whereas molar conductivity decreases on dilution.
 conductivity decreases whereas molar conductivity increases on dilution.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Conductivity depends on the concentration of ions per unit volume of the solution. On dilution this decreases. Molar conductivity increases because with dilution the dissociation of the weak electrolyte increases, causing an increase in the number of active ions in solution.

questions.questionText: Auto-oxidation of chloroform in air and light produces a poisonous gas known as
options:
Phosphine
 Mustard gas
 Phosgene
 Tear gas
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Chloroform undergoes auto oxidation as follows :

$2 \mathrm{CHCl}_3+\underset{\text { (Air) }}{\mathrm{O}_2} \xrightarrow{\text { Light }} \underset{\begin{array}{c}\text { Phosgene } \\ \text { (poisonous gas) }\end{array}}{2 \mathrm{COCl}_2}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): Inversion of configuration is observed in $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reaction.

Reason (R): The reaction proceeds with the formation of carbocation.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason ( $R$ ) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
 Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason ( $R$ ) is not the correct explanation of Assertion (A).
 Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
 Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ mechanism proceeds via a concerted mechanism in which a pentavalent carbon is formed with half bonds in the transition state and no carbocation is formed. There is inversion of configuration due to the attack of the nucleophile from the back.
